title: Nancy.
content:
I.

        Thine am I, my faithful fair,
            Thine, my lovely Nancy;
        Ev'ry pulse along my veins,
            Ev'ry roving fancy.

II.

        To thy bosom lay my heart,
            There to throb and languish:
        Tho' despair had wrung its core,
            That would heal its anguish.

III.

        Take away those rosy lips,
            Rich with balmy treasure:
        Turn away thine eyes of love,
            Lest I die with pleasure.

IV.

        What is life when wanting love?
            Night without a morning:
        Love's the cloudless summer sun,
            Nature gay adorning.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Nancy.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
